speaker: Frank Elderson
tone_summary: Elderson's sole speech in the 8-week window — delivered at the NGFS Annual Plenary in Pretoria — is entirely devoted to the case for incorporating nature-related risks into supervisory practice. He frames the work of the NGFS as evidence that international cooperation remains possible and necessary despite the erosion of the rules-based order. There is zero monetary policy content, no interest rate framing, and no hawkish or dovish signal on monetary conditions. His score of 3.0 reflects not a dovish monetary policy stance but simply the absence of rate-relevant content — he is operating in his supervisory capacity. With only one speech and no monetary policy signal, trend is stable.
representative_quote: Coming together, cooperating and engaging with one another across borders is possible and indeed imperative in times of profound disruption and volatility.
